signals.title: NZD/USD — G10 Bilateral Theme + Geopolitical De-escalation
signals.text: NZD/USD downside pressure intensifies as the USD leads G10 performance (+1.097% 1W avg) while NZD lags (-0.792% 1W avg), creating a 1.889% performance spread. The Israel-Hezbollah ceasefire reduces safe-haven demand for gold, further weighing on the commodity-linked NZD which already faces a -150bp carry disadvantage against the USD. The next RBNZ meeting (mkt: 20% hike priced) on 8 July is the catalyst for determining if the current hike bias can offset this structural weakness.

signals.title: AUD/USD — COT Crowded Long + CESI Divergence
signals.text: AUD COT LF net long +38.1k (68.8% long) creates significant unwind risk as recent Australian economic data consistently disappoints with a CESI net of -8 misses. While the RBA maintains a 4.35% rate with a hold bias, the -72bp OIS carry disadvantage against the USD limits upside potential despite the hawkish rhetoric. The next RBA meeting (mkt: 100% hold priced) on 11 August determines if the policy hold can withstand the deteriorating data trend.

signals.title: USD/JPY — JPY COT Short Squeeze + Yield Gap Resistance
signals.text: JPY COT net short -115.0k (29.2% long) sets up a high-conviction squeeze risk as USD/JPY hovers near 161.28 following a fade in USD momentum. The 290bp US-JGB yield advantage remains the primary structural anchor for the pair, but JPY's strong positive CESI (+17 beats) suggests domestic data outperformance is challenging the carry trade. The next BoJ meeting (mkt: 50% hike priced) on 31 July is the catalyst for a potential hawkish shift to narrow the real rate differential.

signals.title: EUR/USD — COT Short Alignment + Restrictive Real Rate Gap
signals.text: EUR COT net short -49.3k reinforces the downside bias toward 1.14 as market sentiment shifts toward the USD following hawkish Fed signals. The USD maintains a superior real rate of +1.39% compared to the EUR's +0.03%, providing a fundamental yield cushion that offsets the ECB's hiking bias. The next ECB meeting (mkt: 80% hike priced) on 23 July determines if a further hike can compress the +145bp OIS carry disadvantage.

signals.title: USD/CHF — Volatility Risk Premium + Carry Advantage
signals.text: USD/CHF upside momentum is supported by a massive +375bp carry advantage and a significant volatility risk premium (IV 14.3% vs HV30 6.2%). The SNB's dovish stance and mildly accommodative real rate of -0.60% contrast sharply with the Fed's restrictive posture, driving the pair toward seven-month highs. The next SNB meeting (mkt: 100% hold priced) is the catalyst for any intervention rhetoric that might counter the current 1W CHF laggard trend (-0.606% avg).
